svg.text: Dapr PubSub → Flow Config → Python venv → Kafka Consumer → Script Execution
All binary fields base64-encoded in transit
Launched via context.WithCancel() — SIGTERM on flow deletion
├── src/infoblox_driver.py ← Generated from template (imports user script)
├── src/my_audit_script_42.py ← User's script (base64 decoded from flow.Data)
├── src/{interface.py, kafka_consumer.py, soar_light.py, health_checker.py, metrics.py} ← Framework files
→ Sends to Slack / Teams / ServiceNow etc.
acme-corp_42_SOAR_LIGHT_AUDIT_LOG ← One Python process consuming this
acme-corp_42_SOAR_LIGHT_DNS_LOG ← Another Python process consuming this
Consumer group: cdc-soar-light-42 ← Shared group across data types of same flow
Publishes test event → verifies end-to-end delivery
One Python process is spawned PER data type PER flow. Flow with 3 data types = 3 Python processes + 1 health checker thread (in first process alphabetically).
Manager tracks handlers map[string]*Handler keyed by "{flow_id}_{data_type}" — DELETE operation sends SIGTERM to all matching processes.
C2C Upstream — How Events Reach SOAR-Light's Kafka Topics
kafka.flow.processor reads raw topics, filters by ecosystem/dataType, and re-publishes to destination-specific topics including SOAR_LIGHT.
Each flow creates separate Kafka topics per data type — ensuring isolated consumption and offset tracking per flow.
